stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 energy source options. The probability of favorable regional climate data is uncertain, estimated between 41% and 51%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: solar: $130K in good regional climate data, $33K in bad regional climate data. wind: $195K in good regional climate data, $96K in bad regional climate data. natural gas: $120K in good regional climate data, $52K in bad regional climate data. geothermal: $118K in good regional climate data, $42K in bad regional climate data. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.46: best option is wind with EV=$141.5K. wind is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [136.6, 146.5].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
wind is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
